questions.question: QUESTION 1: What are the effects of content exclusions?
options: A. Not available as context
B. Suggestions not available in excluded files
C. Not used while debugging
D. IDE will not count suggestions
questions.explanation: Content exclusions prevent Copilot from using excluded files as context or providing suggestions based on them.

questions.question: QUESTION 2: Which principle emphasizes AI should be understandable?
options: A. Fairness
B. Transparency
C. Inclusiveness
D. Accountability
questions.explanation: Transparency emphasizes that AI systems should be understandable.

questions.question: QUESTION 3: What GitHub Copilot feature can be configured at the organization level to prevent GitHub Copilot suggesting publicly available code snippets?
options: A. GitHub Copilot Chat in the IDE
B. GitHub Copilot Chat in GitHub Mobile
C. GitHub Copilot duplication detection filter
D. GitHub Copilot access to Bing
questions.explanation: The duplication detection filter can be configured at the organization level to prevent GitHub Copilot from suggesting publicly available code snippets.

questions.question: QUESTION 4: What are the potential limitations of GitHub Copilot Chat? (Choose two.)
options: A. Limited training data
B. Ability to handle complex code structures
C. No biases in code suggestions
D. Extensive support for all programming languages
questions.explanation: Copilot Chat has limitations such as limited training data and lack of extensive support for all programming languages.

questions.question: QUESTION 5: What are two techniques that can be used to improve prompts to GitHub Copilot? (Select two.)
options: A. Provide specific success criteria
B. Provide all information about the utilized files
C. Provide insight on where to get the content from to get a response
D. Provide links to supporting documentation
questions.explanation: Improving prompts involves providing specific success criteria and including links to supporting documentation.

questions.question: QUESTION 6: What method can be used to interact with GitHub Copilot?
options: A. By using a properly configured GitHub CLI
B. By using chat capabilities in NeoVim
C. From a watch window in an IDE debug session
D. From a web browser at https://github.copilot.com
questions.explanation: GitHub Copilot integrates into supported IDEs and editors, including NeoVim with the appropriate plugin.

questions.question: QUESTION 7: How does GitHub Copilot Chat utilize its training data and external sources to generate responses?
options: A. It primarily relies on the model's training data
B. It primarily uses search results from Bing
C. It combines its training data set, code in user repositories, and external sources like Bing
D. It uses user-provided documentation exclusively
questions.explanation: Copilot Chat combines its training data, code from user repositories, and external sources like Bing.

questions.question: QUESTION 8: How can you improve the context used by GitHub Copilot? (Choose two.)
options: A. By opening the relevant tabs in your IDE
B. By adding relevant code snippets to your prompt
C. By adding the important files to your .gitconfig
D. By adding the full file paths to your prompt of important files
questions.explanation: Improving context involves opening relevant files in your IDE and adding relevant code snippets to your prompt.

questions.question: QUESTION 9: How can GitHub Copilot be limited when it comes to suggesting unit tests?
options: A. Copilot can generate all types of unit tests
B. Copilot primarily suggests basic unit tests that focus on core functionalities
C. Copilot can handle any complexity in code and automatically generate tests
D. Copilot's limitations vary based on the IDE version
questions.explanation: Copilot often suggests basic unit tests and may not cover edge cases or complex scenarios.

questions.question: QUESTION 10: How do you generate code suggestions with GitHub Copilot in the CLI?
options: A. Write code comments -> Press the suggestion shortcut -> Select the best suggestion
B. Use copilot suggest -> Write the command -> Select the best suggestion
C. Type out the code snippet -> Use copilot refine -> Review the suggestion
D. Describe the project's architecture -> Use copilot generate -> Accept the suggestion
questions.explanation: In the CLI, Copilot generates suggestions by analyzing code comments.

questions.question: QUESTION 11: An independent contractor develops applications for a variety of different customers. Assuming no concerns from their customers, which GitHub Copilot plan is best suited?
options: A. GitHub Copilot Individual
B. GitHub Copilot Business
C. GitHub Copilot Business for non-GHE Customers
D. GitHub Copilot Enterprise
E. GitHub Copilot Teams
questions.explanation: For an independent contractor, GitHub Copilot Individual is the most suitable and cost-effective plan.

questions.question: QUESTION 12: What is a likely effect of GitHub Copilot being trained on commonly used code patterns?
options: A. Suggest homogeneous solutions if provided a diverse data set.
B. Suggest innovative coding solutions that are not yet popular.
C. Suggest code snippets that reflect the most common practices in the training data.
D. Suggest completely novel projects, while reducing time on a project.
questions.explanation: Copilot is likely to suggest code snippets that reflect common practices in its training data.

questions.question: QUESTION 13: What is the best way to share feedback about GitHub Copilot Chat when using it on GitHub Mobile?
options: A. The Settings menu in the GitHub Mobile app.
B. The feedback section on the GitHub website.
C. Use the emojis in the Copilot Chat interface.
D. By tweeting at GitHub's official X account.
questions.explanation: The best way to share feedback on GitHub Mobile is using the emojis in the Copilot Chat interface.

questions.question: QUESTION 14: In what way can GitHub Copilot and GitHub Copilot Chat aid developers in modernizing applications?
options: A. Directly convert legacy applications into cloud-native architectures.
B. Suggest modern programming patterns based on your code.
C. Create and deploy full-stack applications based on a single query.
D. Refactor applications to align with upcoming standards.
questions.explanation: Copilot can analyze existing code and suggest modern programming patterns and best practices.

questions.question: QUESTION 15: How can the concept of fairness be integrated into the process of operating an AI tool?
options: A. Focusing on accessibility will ensure fairness.
B. Collecting large datasets for training will ensure fairness.
C. Regularly monitoring the AI tool's performance will ensure fairness.
D. Training AI data and algorithms to be free from biases will ensure fairness.
questions.explanation: Fairness in AI is achieved by training data and algorithms to be free from biases.

questions.question: QUESTION 16: What is the correct way to exclude specific files from being used by GitHub Copilot Business during code suggestions?
options: A. Modify the .gitignore file to include the specific files.
B. Add the specific files to a copilot.ignore file.
C. Use the GitHub Copilot settings in the user interface to exclude files.
D. Rename the files to include the suffix _no_copilot.
questions.explanation: The correct way is through the GitHub Copilot settings in the UI.

questions.question: QUESTION 17: Why might a Generative AI (Gen AI) tool create inaccurate outputs?
options: A. The tool is overloaded with too many requests.
B. The tool is experiencing downtime.
C. The tool is programmed with a focus on creativity over factual accuracy.
D. The training data might contain biases or inconsistencies.
questions.explanation: Gen AI tools can produce inaccurate outputs if the training data contains biases or inconsistencies.

questions.question: QUESTION 18: Select a strategy to increase the performance of GitHub Copilot Chat.
options: A. Use a single query to resolve all requirements.
B. Limit the number of concurrent users.
C. Apply prompt engineering techniques to be more specific.
D. Optimize memory-intensive operations within generated code.
questions.explanation: Applying prompt engineering techniques to be more specific improves performance and relevance.

questions.question: QUESTION 19: What method can a developer use to generate sample data with GitHub Copilot? (Choose two.)
options: A. Create fictitious information from patterns in training data.
B. Independently initiate and manage data storage services.
C. Directly access and use databases to create sample data.
D. Use suggestions based on API documentation in the repository.
questions.explanation: Copilot can generate sample data by creating fictitious information and using API documentation.

questions.question: QUESTION 20: What is a benefit of using custom models in GitHub Copilot?
options: A. Responses are faster to produce.
B. Responses use practices and patterns in your repositories.
C. Responses use the organization's LLM engine.
D. Responses are guaranteed to be correct.
questions.explanation: Custom models allow Copilot to learn from your repositories, aligning suggestions with your standards.

questions.question: QUESTION 21: Which Copilot Individual features are available when using a supported extension for Visual Studio, VS Code, or JetBrains IDEs? (Choose two.)
options: A. Code suggestions
B. Chat
C. Knowledge Base
D. Pull Request Diff Analysis
questions.explanation: Copilot Individual provides code suggestions and chat features when used with supported IDE extensions.

questions.question: QUESTION 22: What content can be configured to be excluded with content exclusions? (Choose three.)
options: A. Lines in files
B. Files
C. Folders
D. Repositories
E. Gists
questions.explanation: Content exclusions allow you to exclude files, folders, and repositories from being used by Copilot.

questions.question: QUESTION 23: How does the /tests slash command assist developers?
options: A. Constructs detailed test documentation.
B. Creates unit tests for the selected code.
C. Integrates with external testing frameworks.
D. Executes test cases to find issues with the code.
questions.explanation: The /tests slash command creates unit tests for the selected code.

questions.question: QUESTION 24: How long does it take content exclusion to add or be updated?
options: A. Up to 30 minutes
B. 45 - 60 minutes
C. 60 - 90 minutes
D. 24 hours
questions.explanation: Content exclusions typically take up to 30 minutes to be added or updated.

questions.question: QUESTION 25: What is the correct way to access the audit log events for GitHub Copilot Business?
options: A. Navigate to the Security tab in the organization's GitHub settings
B. Navigate to the Insights tab in the repository settings
C. Use the Audit log section in the organization's GitHub settings
D. Use the Code tab in the GitHub repository
questions.explanation: Audit log events can be accessed through the Audit log section in the organization's GitHub settings.

questions.question: QUESTION 26: Which prompts can be used to guide GitHub Copilot Chat in refactoring code for quality improvements? (Choose two.)
options: A. Show me how to improve the readability of this function.
B. Suggest ways to enhance the maintainability of this code segment.
C. Refactor my application to meet the latest coding standards.
D. Predict future coding trends and update my codebase accordingly.
questions.explanation: Effective prompts for refactoring include requests that focus on readability and maintainability.

questions.question: QUESTION 27: Which of the following is correct about GitHub Copilot Knowledge Bases?
options: A. All repos are indexed
B. Indexing is static
C. It is an Enterprise feature
D. All file types are indexed
questions.explanation: GitHub Copilot Knowledge Bases is an Enterprise feature.

questions.question: QUESTION 28: How does GitHub Copilot Chat ensure that a function works correctly?
options: A. By suggesting assertions based on the code's context and semantics.
B. By automatically writing all the tests for the function.
C. By writing the implementation code for the function.
D. By executing the test cases to validate correctness.
questions.explanation: Copilot Chat can suggest assertions based on the code's context and semantics.

questions.question: QUESTION 29: How can GitHub Copilot assist developers during the requirements analysis phase of the SDLC?
options: A. Automatically generating detailed requirements documents.
B. Providing templates and code snippets that help in documenting requirements.
C. Identifying and fixing potential requirement conflicts when using /help.
D. Managing stakeholder communication and meetings.
questions.explanation: Copilot can provide templates and code snippets that aid in documenting requirements.

questions.question: QUESTION 30: Why is code reviewing still necessary when using GitHub Copilot to write tests?
options: A. Because Copilot can cover all possible scenarios in your test cases.
B. Because Copilot generates the best code possible for the test scenario.
C. Because Copilot's generated test cases may not cover all possible scenarios.
D. Because Copilot replaces the need for manual testing.
questions.explanation: Code review is necessary because Copilot's generated test cases might not cover all possible scenarios.

questions.question: QUESTION 31: Which statement best describes the impact of GitHub Copilot on the software development process?
options: A. It decreases software vulnerabilities from third party dependencies.
B. It reduces overhead by automating testing workflows.
C. It increases productivity by automating repetitive coding tasks.
D. It replaces the need for developers in the software development process.
questions.explanation: Copilot increases productivity by automating repetitive coding tasks.

questions.question: QUESTION 32: Identify the right use cases where GitHub Copilot Chat is most effective. (Choose two.)
options: A. Create a technical requirement specification from business documentation
B. Explain legacy COBOL code and translate to Python
C. Create a unit test scenario for newly developed Python code
D. Create end-to-end performance testing scenarios for a web application
questions.explanation: Copilot Chat is effective for explaining and translating legacy code and generating unit test scenarios.

questions.question: QUESTION 33: Which scenario best describes the intended use of GitHub Copilot Chat?
options: A. A complete replacement for developers generating code.
B. A productivity tool that provides suggestions, but relying on human judgment.
C. A solution requiring no additional input or oversight.
D. A tool solely designed for debugging and error correction.
questions.explanation: Copilot Chat is a productivity enhancer, not a replacement for developers.

questions.question: QUESTION 34: How does GitHub Copilot Chat help in understanding the existing codebase?
options: A. By running code linters and formatters.
B. By providing visual diagrams of the code structure.
C. By answering questions about the code and generating explanations.
D. By automatically refactoring code to improve readability.
questions.explanation: Copilot Chat helps by answering questions about the code and generating explanations.

questions.question: QUESTION 35: What should developers consider when relying on Copilot for statistical analysis?
options: A. Copilot can independently verify statistical significance.
B. Copilot's suggestions are based on trends and may not apply accurately to specific datasets.
C. Copilot will automatically correct statistical errors.
D. Copilot can design new statistical methods.
questions.explanation: Copilot's suggestions are based on trends and may not always be accurate for specific datasets.

questions.question: QUESTION 36: What is a key consideration when relying on Copilot Chat's explanations?
options: A. Explanations are dynamically updated based on feedback.
B. Reviewing and validating the generated output for accuracy and completeness.
C. Copilot Chat uses a static database for explanations.
D. Explanations are primarily derived from user-provided documentation.
questions.explanation: It's crucial to review and validate Copilot Chat's generated output.

questions.question: QUESTION 37: Where is the proxy service hosted?
options: A. Self hosted
B. Amazon Web Service
C. Microsoft Azure
D. Google Cloud Platform
questions.explanation: The proxy service for GitHub Copilot is hosted on Microsoft Azure.

questions.question: QUESTION 38: What is zero-shot prompting?
options: A. Giving as little context as possible
B. Telling Copilot to show only the correct answer
C. Only giving Copilot a question as a prompt and no examples
D. Giving Copilot examples of the problem
E. Giving Copilot examples of the algorithm and outcome
questions.explanation: Zero-shot prompting involves asking Copilot a question without providing examples.

questions.question: QUESTION 39: What GitHub Copilot pricing plan gives access to company knowledge bases?
options: A. GitHub Copilot Individual
B. GitHub Copilot Business
C. GitHub Copilot Enterprise
D. GitHub Copilot Professional
questions.explanation: Copilot Enterprise provides access to company knowledge bases.

questions.question: QUESTION 40: How can Copilot facilitate learning a new programming language? (Choose two.)
options: A. Copilot Chat can provide guidance for common tasks.
B. Copilot's /understand command helps Copilot understand code.
C. Copilot can provide contextualized suggestions from documentation.
D. Copilot can convert comments into code to grasp syntax.
questions.explanation: Copilot helps by guiding common tasks and converting comments into code.

questions.question: QUESTION 41: How long does GitHub retain Copilot data for Business and Enterprise? (Choose two.)
options: A. Prompts and Suggestions: Not retained
B. Prompts and Suggestions: Retained for 28 days
C. User Engagement Data: Kept for Two Years
D. User Engagement Data: Kept for One Year
questions.explanation: Prompts and suggestions are retained for 28 days; user engagement data is kept for two years.

questions.question: QUESTION 42: Which of the following describes role prompting?
options: A. Describing in your prompt what your role is to get a better suggestion
B. Telling Copilot in what tone of voice it should respond
C. Prompt Copilot to explain the role of a suggestion
D. Giving Copilot multiple examples of the form of the data
questions.explanation: Role prompting involves explicitly stating your role in the prompt to guide Copilot's responses.

questions.question: QUESTION 43: What practices enhance the quality of suggestions provided by GitHub Copilot? (Select three.)
options: A. Clearly defining the problem or task
B. Including personal information in the code comments
C. Using meaningful variable names
D. Providing examples of desired output
E. Use a .gitignore file to exclude irrelevant files
questions.explanation: The quality of Copilot's suggestions is enhanced by clearly defining the task, using meaningful variable names, and providing examples of desired output.

questions.question: QUESTION 44: Which REST API endpoint is used to modify details about a GitHub Copilot Business subscription? (Choose two.)
options: A. Add teams to the Copilot subscription for an organization
B. Upgrade or downgrade the subscription tier
C. Migrate Copilot seat assignments between GitHub organizations
D. Reassign Copilot seats based on repository size
E. Remove teams from the Copilot subscription for an organization
questions.explanation: The REST API endpoints are used to add and remove teams from the Copilot Business subscription.

questions.question: QUESTION 45: How can GitHub Copilot assist with code refactoring tasks?
options: A. Fix syntax errors without user input
B. Automatically rewrite code to follow best practices
C. Suggest refactoring improvements for better code quality
D. Remove unnecessary files from the project directory
questions.explanation: Copilot can analyze existing code and suggest refactoring improvements to enhance quality.

questions.question: QUESTION 46: What is the primary role of the /optimize slash command in Visual Studio?
options: A. Automatically formats the code according to the style guide
B. Enhances performance by analyzing runtime complexity
C. Summarizes documentation into more maintainable formats
D. Translates code into a more performant language
questions.explanation: The /optimize command enhances performance by analyzing runtime complexity and suggesting improvements.

questions.question: QUESTION 47: How can you use GitHub Copilot to get inline suggestions for refactoring your code? (Select two.)
options: A. By adding comments to your code and triggering a suggestion
B. By highlighting code, right-clicking, and selecting 'Fix using GitHub Copilot'
C. By running the gh copilot fix command
D. By using the '/fix' command in Copilot inline chat
E. By highlighting code, right-clicking, and selecting 'Refactor using GitHub Copilot'
questions.explanation: You can trigger inline refactoring suggestions by adding comments or using the 'Refactor using GitHub Copilot' option.

questions.question: QUESTION 48: What is a limitation of content exclusions?
options: A. Content exclusions can be worked around as it is only available for Git repositories.
B. Content exclusions can only be configured by an enterprise administrator.
C. Content exclusions are only available in the Copilot Individual plan.
D. Repository administrators and organization owners cannot manage content exclusion settings.
questions.explanation: Content exclusions can be worked around since they are only available for Git repositories.

questions.question: QUESTION 49: Which of the following is a risk associated with using AI?
options: A. AI algorithms are incapable of perpetuating existing biases.
B. AI systems can sometimes make decisions that are difficult to interpret.
C. AI eliminates the need for data privacy regulations.
D. AI replaces the need for developer opportunities in most fields.
questions.explanation: AI systems can sometimes make decisions that are difficult to interpret.

questions.question: QUESTION 50: What can the API track or report?
options: A. Generate detailed reports on code quality improvements
B. Track acceptance rate of code suggestions used in the organization
C. Refactor code to improve productivity
D. Provide feedback on coding style and standards compliance
E. Provide Copilot Chat specific suggestions acceptance metrics
questions.explanation: The API can track acceptance rate of suggestions and provide Copilot Chat-specific metrics.

questions.question: QUESTION 51: A company is storing code in Bitbucket and wants to use Copilot. Which plan is most cost effective to manage users with an Identity Provider?
options: A. Copilot Business for non-GHE customers
B. Copilot Individual
C. Copilot Enterprise
D. Copilot Teams
questions.explanation: Copilot Enterprise is the most cost-effective plan for managing users with an Identity Provider.

questions.question: QUESTION 52: Which Copilot Business activities can be tracked using organization audit logs?
options: A. Accepted chat suggestions
B. Code suggestions made by Copilot
C. Changes to content exclusion settings
D. Suggestions blocked by duplication detection filtering
questions.explanation: Audit logs can track changes to content exclusion settings.

questions.question: QUESTION 53: Copilot in CLI can configure which settings? (Choose two.)
options: A. The default execution confirmation
B. Usage analytics
C. The default editor
D. GitHub CLI subcommands
questions.explanation: Copilot CLI can configure default execution confirmation and usage analytics.

questions.question: QUESTION 54: How does Copilot Enterprise assist in code reviews during pull requests? (Select two.)
options: A. Automatically merges pull requests
B. Generates a prose summary and bulleted list of key changes
C. Validates accuracy of changes
D. Answers questions about the changeset
questions.explanation: Copilot Enterprise generates summaries and can answer questions about the changeset.

questions.question: QUESTION 55: What is the process behind identifying public code matches with the public code filter?
options: A. Running code suggestions through filters designed to detect public code
B. Comparing suggestions against public code using machine learning
C. Analyzing context and structure of the code
D. Reviewing user's browsing history to identify public repositories
questions.explanation: Public code matches are identified by running suggestions through filters designed to detect public code.

questions.question: QUESTION 56: What are potential risks of relying heavily on Copilot-generated code? (Choose two.)
options: A. Copilot may introduce security vulnerabilities
B. Copilot may decrease developer velocity due to prompt engineering
C. Copilot's suggestions may not reflect best practices or latest standards
D. Copilot may increase lead time with irrelevant suggestions
questions.explanation: Copilot may introduce vulnerabilities and its suggestions may not always reflect best practices.

questions.question: QUESTION 57: Which Copilot plan allows for prompt and suggestion collection?
options: A. Copilot Individual
B. Copilot Business
C. Copilot Enterprise
D. Copilot Codespace
questions.explanation: Copilot Enterprise allows for prompt and suggestion collection.

questions.question: QUESTION 58: How does Copilot suggest code optimizations for performance?
options: A. By analyzing the codebase and suggesting efficient algorithms or data structures
B. By automatically rewriting the codebase
C. By enforcing strict coding standards
D. By providing detailed performance reports
questions.explanation: Copilot suggests optimizations by analyzing code and recommending efficient algorithms or data structures.

questions.question: QUESTION 59: What types of prompts or code snippets might be flagged by the Copilot toxicity filter? (Choose two.)
options: A. Hate speech or discriminatory language
B. Sexually suggestive or explicit content
C. Code with logical errors
D. Code comments with strong opinions
questions.explanation: Copilot's toxicity filter flags prompts containing hate speech or sexually explicit content.

questions.question: QUESTION 60: Which Microsoft ethical AI principle is aimed at ensuring AI systems treat all people equally?
options: A. Privacy and Security
B. Fairness
C. Reliability and Safety
D. Inclusiveness
questions.explanation: The principle of fairness ensures that AI systems treat all people equally and avoid discriminatory outcomes.

questions.question: QUESTION 61: How is GitHub Copilot Individual billed? (Choose two.)
options: A. Monthly as a subscription
B. Annually as a subscription
C. Monthly, as a metered service based on actual consumption
D. Free (not billed) for all open source projects
questions.explanation: Copilot Individual is billed as a monthly or annual subscription.

questions.question: QUESTION 62: How can you get multiple suggestions from GitHub Copilot?
options: A. By asking for multiple suggestions using comments
B. By opening the completions panel in your editor
C. By using the inline chat command 'multiple'
D. By using @workspace in the chat window
questions.explanation: You can get multiple suggestions by opening the completions panel in your editor.

questions.question: QUESTION 63: Which of the following are key features of GitHub Copilot Chat?
options: A. Automatically refactors entire codebases
B. Generates detailed test cases based on prompts
C. Collaborates in real-time with other developers
D. Provides step-by-step explanations for suggested code snippets
questions.explanation: Copilot Chat provides step-by-step explanations for suggested code snippets.

questions.question: QUESTION 64: Best practice when Copilot suggests unfamiliar code?
options: A. Use as placeholder and refactor later
B. Assume suggestions are optimized and secure
C. Manually review the suggested code to understand logic
D. Accept without review
questions.explanation: Best practice is to manually review Copilot's suggestions before integrating them.

questions.question: QUESTION 65: Which features are offered by GitHub Copilot Chat? (Select three.)
options: A. Detailed explanations of complex code snippets
B. Context-aware coding suggestions
C. Real-time collaboration with team members
D. Ability to ask questions about the codebase
E. Automated bug detection and correction
questions.explanation: Copilot Chat offers explanations, context-aware suggestions, and the ability to ask questions about the codebase.

questions.question: QUESTION 66: What is a key benefit of using Copilot in a team environment?
options: A. Automatically merges all pull requests
B. Encourages code consistency by offering similar suggestions
C. Generates team standup notes
D. Replaces the need for code review
questions.explanation: Copilot encourages code consistency by offering similar suggestions to team members.

questions.question: QUESTION 67: What does inline suggestion mean in Copilot?
options: A. Suggestions sent via email
B. Code rewritten silently in background
C. Suggestions appear within the editor as you type
D. Copilot runs code and suggests output
questions.explanation: Inline suggestions appear directly in the editor as you type.

questions.question: QUESTION 68: How can you improve the accuracy of Copilot's suggestions?
options: A. Avoid using comments
B. Provide descriptive function names and comments
C. Write only pseudocode
D. Run Copilot in offline mode
questions.explanation: Providing descriptive names and comments improves Copilot's accuracy.

questions.question: QUESTION 69: What best describes Copilot's default behavior for data privacy in Business?
options: A. Code suggestions are never stored
B. Suggestions may be shared across repositories
C. Suggestions are filtered to avoid matching public code
D. Training data is updated in real-time
questions.explanation: Copilot Business filters suggestions to avoid matching public code.

questions.question: QUESTION 70: What limitation should developers keep in mind when using Copilot?
options: A. Requires Windows OS
B. Suggestions may include insecure or deprecated patterns
C. Guarantees 100% test coverage
D. Only works for Python and JavaScript
questions.explanation: Copilot suggestions may include insecure or deprecated patterns.

questions.question: QUESTION 71: Why use Copilot Chat over inline suggestions?
options: A. Chat writes entire projects without input
B. Chat can explain code, suggest fixes, and offer contextual help
C. Chat disables access to public repositories
D. Inline suggestions are only for JavaScript
questions.explanation: Copilot Chat can explain code, suggest fixes, and provide contextual help beyond inline completion.

questions.question: QUESTION 72: Which best practice ensures Copilot generates high-quality code?
options: A. Use vague function names
B. Rely entirely on suggestions without review
C. Prompt Copilot with specific goals and expected outcomes
D. Avoid importing libraries
questions.explanation: Prompting Copilot with specific goals and expected outcomes ensures higher quality suggestions.

questions.question: QUESTION 73: In Copilot Business, how can an administrator manage access at scale?
options: A. Enable access only through SSH
B. Assign seats to users or teams via organization settings
C. Install Copilot manually for each user
D. Request tokens from GitHub support
questions.explanation: Administrators manage access by assigning seats to users or teams via organization settings.

questions.question: QUESTION 74: How does Copilot handle licensing of its code suggestions?
options: A. All suggestions are under MIT license
B. Copilot labels each suggestion with its license
C. Suggestions are not guaranteed to be license-free or unique
D. All Copilot-generated code is open source by default
questions.explanation: Copilot suggestions are not guaranteed to be license-free or unique.

questions.question: QUESTION 75: What is one way to reduce Copilot's relevance in a specific project?
options: A. Use keyboard shortcuts to disable suggestions
B. Create a .copilot-ignore file to exclude files or directories
C. Delete the .gitignore file
D. Set the repository to public
questions.explanation: You can reduce Copilot's relevance by creating a .copilot-ignore file to exclude files or directories.

questions.question: QUESTION 76: What are the effects of content exclusions?
options: A. Not available as context
B. Suggestions not available in excluded files
C. Not used while debugging
D. IDE will not count suggestions
questions.explanation: Content exclusions prevent Copilot from using excluded files as context or providing suggestions based on them.

questions.question: QUESTION 77: What kind of insights can the GitHub Copilot usage metrics API provide to help evaluate the effectiveness of GitHub Copilot? (Choose two.)
options: A. The API can generate detailed reports on code quality improvements made by GitHub Copilot.
B. The API can track the number of code suggestions accepted and used in the organization.
C. The API can refactor your code to improve productivity.
D. The API can provide feedback on coding style and standards compliance.
E. The API can provide Copilot Chat specific suggestions acceptance metrics.
questions.explanation: The usage metrics API helps evaluate Copilot effectiveness by tracking accepted suggestions across the organization and providing Copilot Chat-specific acceptance metrics.

questions.question: QUESTION 78: Which of the following GitHub Copilot Business related activities can be tracked using the organization audit logs?
options: A. Accepted chat suggestions
B. Code suggestions made by GitHub Copilot
C. Changes to content exclusion settings
D. Suggestions blocked by duplication detection filtering
questions.explanation: Organization audit logs can track changes to content exclusion settings, helping administrators monitor and manage Copilot Business configurations.

questions.question: QUESTION 79: What configuration needs to be set to get help from Microsoft and GitHub protecting against IP infringement while using GitHub Copilot?
options: A. Suggestions matching public code to ‘blocked’
B. Enforce blocking of MIT or GPL licensed code
C. You need to check code suggestions yourself before accepting
D. Enabled GitHub Copilot license checking
questions.explanation: To protect against IP infringement, you need to configure GitHub Copilot to block suggestions that match public code.

questions.question: QUESTION 80: Which GitHub Copilot plan allows for prompt and suggestion collection?
options: A. GitHub Copilot Individuals
B. GitHub Copilot Business
C. GitHub Copilot Enterprise
D. GitHub Copilot Codespace
questions.explanation: Prompt and suggestion collection is available with the GitHub Copilot Individuals plan.
